title: Coverage ledger: >= 60% standards in one grade-band x subject have a reviewed OER
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. The coverage ledger turns the record set into an outcome metric: what fraction of a slice's standards now have a reviewed aligned OER.
objective: Produce the coverage ledger showing >= 60% of standards in one grade-band x subject have >= 1 reviewed aligned OER.
acceptanceCriteria: Coverage ledger computes, for at least one grade-band x subject, the percentage of standards with >= 1 reviewed aligned OER and records >= 60%.
Ledger is regenerable from the record set; method and denominator (standard count) documented.
Published with the dataset release; Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; the commit is DCO signed-off.
